headline: JPMorgan Chase profit hits $16.9 billion in the second quarter, boosted again by market volatility
summary: JPMorgan Chase said Tuesday that it logged $16.9 billion in second-quarter profit as its equities trading division again took advantage of market volatility triggered by the war in Iran.  The nation's largest bank by asset size, JPMorgan said that revenue in every line of its business hit record levels in the quarter, including its markets division, where revenue grew 35% over the same period last year.  Revenue in its equity markets division skyrocketed 86%.

content.title: JPMorgan Chase profit hits $16.9 billion in the second quarter, boosted again by market volatility

The nation's largest bank by asset size, JPMorgan said that revenue in every line of its business hit record levels in the quarter, including its markets division, where revenue grew 35% over the same period last year.  Revenue in its equity markets division skyrocketed 86%.

headline: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) Delivers Impressive Q2 CY2026
summary: Global financial services giant JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q2 CY2026, with sales up 27% year on year to $58.02 billion. Its GAAP profit of $7.70 per share was 31.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: ‘Extraordinary’ Earnings Results Lift Bank Stocks
summary: Bank stocks were rising Tuesday morning after the nation’s largest banks reported strong second-quarter earnings due to powerhouse results from investment banking units which have reaped the benefits of a surge in dealmaking activity.  “We thought the 2Q earnings were going to be very good, but they turned out to be extraordinary,” Macrae Sykes, portfolio manager of the GABF ETF at Gabelli Funds, said in a statement.  The KBW Bank Index was up 1.75%, while the benchmark S&P 500 index was up 0.28%.

The KBW Bank Index was up 1.75%, while the benchmark S&P 500 index was up 0.28%.

headline: Buoyant Stock Market Boosts Wealth Units at Major Banks
summary: Wealth management units at the nation’s largest banks reported rising client assets for the second quarter, thanks to a combination of market appreciation and asset-gathering.  More money in client investment accounts is a boon for the fees that the wealth units generate on assets under management.  Citigroup reported client investment assets rose 14% year over year to $727 billion.

headline: JPMorgan Boosts Outlook for Closely Watched Net Interest Income
summary: JPMorgan Chase, the largest U.S. bank by assets, raised its net interest income outlook for 2026, saying it now expects full-year net interest income of some $105.5 billion, depending on market conditions, or $96.5 billion excluding the bank’s markets business.  Investors closely track net interest income, or NII, as both a core measure of banks’ profitability in their core function—lending—and as a significant revenue driver.  NII represents the sum that banks generate from interest-earning assets, such as loans and mortgages, less what they pay back out to customers.

content.title: JPMorgan Boosts Outlook for Closely Watched Net Interest Income
content.summary: JPMorgan Chase, the largest U.S. bank by assets, raised its net interest income outlook for 2026, saying it now expects full-year net interest income of some $105.5 billion, depending on market conditions, or $96.5 billion excluding the bank’s markets business.  Investors closely track net interest income, or NII, as both a core measure of banks’ profitability in their core function—lending—and as a significant revenue driver.  NII represents the sum that banks generate from interest-earning assets, such as loans and mortgages, less what they pay back out to customers.

headline: JPMorgan Expects Higher Expenses, Lower Charge-Offs This Year
summary: JPMorgan Chase adjusted its full-year outlook on Tuesday with a mix of positive and negative news, sending shares lower by 2.5% in premarket trading.  Driving that increase is “higher volume- and revenue-related expenses driven by the activity levels and associated revenue outperformance.”  JPMorgan excludes firmwide legal costs in its definition of adjusted expenses.

headline: Bank Earnings Show Consumers Are Still in Good Shape
summary: Mike Santomassimo, the bank’s CFO, attributed the positive credit trends to the U.S. economy and consumers remaining resilient even in the face of stickier-than-expected inflation.  Santomassimo attributed that sturdiness to a relatively stable jobs environment.  “We are not seeing cracks,” Santomassimo said on a call with reporters.

headline: Dimon Backs JPMorgan’s New Consumer-Banking Chief After Analyst Questions Experience
summary: Mike Mayo, the widely-followed bank analyst at Wells Fargo, has grilled JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon on quarterly earnings calls for years.  JPMorgan’s call with analysts on Tuesday was no exception.  Mayo asked Dimon why he was confident that Troy Rohrbaugh, the newly named bank co-president and chief of the vast Chase consumer- and community-banking business, was the right person for the role.

Mayo asked Dimon why he was confident that Troy Rohrbaugh, the newly named bank co-president and chief of the vast Chase consumer- and community-banking business, was the right person for the role.

headline: Banks rake in big profits as both Wall Street and the US consumer stay strong
summary: Five of the biggest U.S. banks reported record profits on Tuesday, boosted by their trading desks and a remarkably resilient American consumer amid persistent global economic uncertainty.  It marks the second straight quarter of strong results from the banks, which have benefited from market volatility since the Iran war began in late February.  Both consumer-facing and market-focused banks reported top- and bottom-line growth that beat even the most optimistic Wall Street expectations.

content.title: Banks rake in big profits as both Wall Street and the US consumer stay strong
content.summary: Five of the biggest U.S. banks reported record profits on Tuesday, boosted by their trading desks and a remarkably resilient American consumer amid persistent global economic uncertainty.  It marks the second straight quarter of strong results from the banks, which have benefited from market volatility since the Iran war began in late February.  Both consumer-facing and market-focused banks reported top- and bottom-line growth that beat even the most optimistic Wall Street expectations.

headline: Review & Preview: Inflation Cools
summary: It was a jam-packed day for the markets: earnings season started strong, oil futures fluctuated, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh testified in front of lawmakers, and the latest June inflation report delivered a cooler-than-expected reading that should make policymakers hold off on raising interest rates—for now.  The Dow closed just above the flatline at 0.02%.  The Nasdaq Composite was up 0.9% and the S&P 500 finished the day up 0.4%.

content.title: Review & Preview: Inflation Cools
content.summary: It was a jam-packed day for the markets: earnings season started strong, oil futures fluctuated, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh testified in front of lawmakers, and the latest June inflation report delivered a cooler-than-expected reading that should make policymakers hold off on raising interest rates—for now.  The Dow closed just above the flatline at 0.02%.  The Nasdaq Composite was up 0.9% and the S&P 500 finished the day up 0.4%.
